author: @raghavkhandelwal349
text: The copper suspension that you've made here, is it resistant to oxidation post printing?

author: @stzeybek
text: Hello sir, do you have any tutorials for injection conductive copper ink in to cartridges?

author: @edspencer5122
text: I am not a chemist so much of hard work is wasted.  I am thinking of trying to make a conductive paint to use on my sea shells ect. so I can electroform from a copper plating bath.  Can't wait to get my cellulose acetate to dissolve with acetone, add the metallic copper to make conductive paint, and paint on my sea shell substrates.  Thanks again, very much, for all your science....

author: @monzavada
text: Hello, I really love your work and the way you explain things. Just amazing. I just wanted to know if this ink will print on plastic surface. TIA

author: @morganparkman2708
text: Hello! Is this the same compound used in other vidoes for the rub on copper plating?

author: @ШихнабиНабиев
text: If mix ascorbic acid and copper sulfate will be a green solution, but you have a different color. Why? Thank you.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: different temp and different concentrations - review the video using glycol

author: @ШихнабиНабиев
text:
Dear Robert Murray-Smith.
What materials do you apply for make Copper Nanoparticles Suitable For Ink Jet Printing? Thank you.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I did a video on it mate

author: @DeliciousDeBlair
text: Wow what an excellent way to print circuits! (~_^)-b

author: @Vibranium603
text: Why does CuO nanoparticle ink require higher energy to be sintered when compared to Ag nanoparticle ink?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Arvind G you have to reduce it to copper

author: @TheViennaThomas
text: What size would these nanoparticles be, approximately?

replies.author: @TheViennaThomas
replies.text: Also, how would one go about making this with ammonia instead?

author: @paulhaarup3360
text: I very much appreciate your videos. Although your British accent is wonderful, as a "yank," I do well just to discern the deep Texas drawl. Kind sir, would you please list the first two chemicals used in this presentation?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Paul Haarup ascorbic acid and copper sulphate

replies.author: @paulhaarup3360
replies.text:
Thank you so very kindly for your reply. In the video, you initially spoke of two less common chemicals that (forgive my spelling) sounded like "Ethylene Diamene" and "Monoethylemene." Those two have tested hearing, repeatedly.

We leave in a month to spend three weeks in England, Scotland and Wales, caravaning. My ability to understand proper English shall be honed.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Paul Haarup those two are amines that complex the copper - ammonia will do the same thing but it stinks to high heaven - it helps improve copper ion concentration

replies.author: @pahaarup
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith Thank you for your time, sir.

author: @Palomamoic
text: Hey Robert, I sent you an email asking a couple of questions. I love your videos !

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Paloma Carvalho i will have a look and i am glad you like the vids

author: @ArkiverUnifiedEnergy
text:
The end result color of the solution reminds me of the oxide of a home made photocell from superheated copper. Without sintering does this generate voltage from light in contact with graphene or a metal?
Your previous video has me wondering if the light in the room is creating a self electroplating solution.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: do you know mate - i don't know but that is a very good question - if it was the same as a copper oxide photocell it would be brick red by the way

replies.author: @ArkiverUnifiedEnergy
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith After seeing the results of your experiments I think it might generate voltage. Don't know for sure. Our perception of color could be altered by concentration and background.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Michael Meadows
definitely worth a look at mate

author: @EddieTheGrouch
text:
I can't help but to think of you as the *Graham Kerr of Chemistry*.
Eagerly awaiting an electroluminescent ink jet version! :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - thanks - i think! - i'll get EL soon enough lol - actually more like when i can lol
